description:
You are a junior log-analyst who has just received a freshly-provisioned Linux host.  
Your team lead asks you to capture a quick, point-in-time snapshot of three basic network parameters so that an automated checker can later compare the host’s current state to the snapshot.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/network_logs  
   if it does **not** already exist.

2. Inside that directory create **exactly one** plain-text file named  
   /home/user/network_logs/network_diagnostics.log

3. Populate that file with **the literal, verbatim output** of the following three commands, in the exact order shown.  
   For each command, precede its output with the section header shown below (the header must be placed on its own line and must match the capitalization and punctuation exactly).  
   Insert **one single blank line** after every section (including after the last one).  
   No extra lines or spaces are allowed anywhere else in the file.

   Command list and required section headers:

   a) Header line:  
      === IPv4 Addresses ===  
      Command whose output must follow:  
      ip -4 addr show

   b) Header line:  
      === Routing Table ===  
      Command whose output must follow:  
      ip route show

   c) Header line:  
      === Listening TCP and UDP Ports ===  
      Command whose output must follow:  
      ss -lntu

4. When you are finished, cat the file to the terminal so that a human reviewer can visually confirm its contents.

Summary of required file layout (blank lines shown as ␤ for clarity):

=== IPv4 Addresses ===␤  
<exact output from “ip -4 addr show”>␤  
␤  
=== Routing Table ===␤  
<exact output from “ip route show”>␤  
␤  
=== Listening TCP and UDP Ports ===␤  
<exact output from “ss -lntu”>␤  
␤  

The automated tests will verify:

• The directory /home/user/network_logs/ exists.  
• The file /home/user/network_logs/network_diagnostics.log exists.  
• The file contains all three headers in the correct order.  
• Exactly one blank line follows each header+command block.  
• No extra characters or whitespace appear before the first header or after the final blank line.  
• Each header is immediately followed by the true, unmodified output of its corresponding command as executed on the container at runtime.

Do not display or save any other data. Perform all steps as an ordinary (non-root) user.
truth:
expected_directory: /home/user/network_logs
expected_file: /home/user/network_logs/network_diagnostics.log
required_headers_in_order:
  1: "=== IPv4 Addresses ==="
  2: "=== Routing Table ==="
  3: "=== Listening TCP and UDP Ports ==="
blank_line_regex: ^\s*$
file_must_end_with_single_blank_line: true
no_leading_or_trailing_whitespace_outside_sections: true
verification_steps:
  - Ensure directory exists and is a directory.
  - Ensure file exists and is a regular file.
  - Read entire file; split by newline.
  - Confirm first non-empty line is header 1.
  - Confirm headers appear in the exact order listed above.
  - After each header+its dynamic content block, expect exactly one completely empty line.
  - After last blank line, expect end-of-file (no extra characters or whitespace).
  - Runtime-capture fresh outputs of:
        ip -4 addr show
        ip route show
        ss -lntu
    and compare them byte-for-byte to the corresponding blocks stored in the file.
name: task_000000_3553ac20
